hypothesis: Incorporating ternary metal combinations (two distinct metal species) into the chemical template framework will yield stable superhydrides with non-integer hydrogen-to-metal ratios exhibiting Tc values exceeding 200 K at moderate pressures (below 150 GPa), because the electronic heterogeneity introduced by mixed metal sublattices will create synergistic quasi-atom interstitial sites that simultaneously optimize hydrogen cage geometry and electron-phonon coupling strength beyond what any single-metal template can achieve.
